Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.model

Class ComputerPlayer

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.model.AbstractPlayer
com.example._0tazo.model.ComputerPlayer
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPlayer
public class ComputerPlayer extends AbstractPlayer
Represents an AI-controlled player in the Cincuentazo card game.

Card selection is performed automatically by chooseCard(int), which applies a greedy strategy: among all legally playable cards, it picks the one whose value brings the table sum closest to 50 without exceeding it, maximizing pressure on the next player.

Cards drawn by computer players are always dealt face down, as required by the game rules.

The actual turn delay (2â€“4 s for playing, 1â€“2 s for drawing) is handled by a Thread or Timeline in the controller â€” not here, to keep the model free of JavaFX dependencies.

  • Constructor Details

    • ComputerPlayer

      public ComputerPlayer(String name)
      Constructs a computer player with the given display name.
      Parameters:
      name - the player's display name (e.g. "Machine 1")

  • Method Details

    • chooseCard

      public int chooseCard(int currentSum)
      Selects the index of the best legal card to play given the current table sum.

      Strategy: among all cards whose value would not cause the sum to exceed 50, choose the one that results in the highest new sum, putting maximum pressure on the next player. Ties are broken by the lowest hand index.

      Parameters:
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      the hand index (0â€“3) of the chosen card, or -1 if no legal move exists

    • isFaceUpDraw

      public boolean isFaceUpDraw()
      Computer player always draws cards face down.
      Returns:
      false

    • getPlayableIndices

      public List<Integer> getPlayableIndices(int currentSum)
      Returns a list of all hand indices that are legally playable given the current table sum.

      Useful for the controller when animating or logging the set of options the computer evaluated before choosing.

      Parameters:
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      a list of playable hand indices; empty if no legal move exists
